back_translation: I, the church-elder, to beloved Gaius, the one whom I love in the truth
Beloved, I pray that you may prosper in everything, and have good health, just as your soul also is prospering
Because I am very glad when some brothers came and testified about the truth in you, namely that you are walking in the truth
I have no joy any greater than hearing that my children all are walking in the truth
Beloved, you have done what is faithful, in whatever you have done for the brothers, and especially for strangers
They have testified about your love before the church. You will do well if you send them onward in a manner that is worthy of God
Because they went out for the Name, not receiving anything from the Gentiles
Therefore we must support such people, so that we will become fellow-workers with the truth
I have already written something to the church, but Diotrephes, the one who is seeking primacy among them, does not accept us

Therefore, if I come, I will rebuke him about his deeds, that he uses evil words to slander us. And he is not satisfied with merely that. He still does not receive the brothers, and forbids those who want to receive, and even casts them out of the church
Beloved, do not imitate evil things, but imitate good things. The one who does good is from God. The one who does evil has not seen God at all
Demetrius has received good testimony from everyone, and from the truth itself also. We ourselves are also witnesses, and you also know that our testimony is the truth
I have many things to write to you, but I do not want to write to you with ink and pen
But I hope that I will see you soon, and we will be able to speak face-to-face
May peace be with you. The friends all send greetings to you. May you send greetings to the friends all individually
